query: When was the Eiffel Tower built? Include the exact construction dates and year.
answer:
**The Eiffel Tower's construction began with the first digging work on January 26, 1887, and the main structural work was completed on March 31, 1889, taking 2 years, 2 months, and 5 days.**[1][3][5][6]

Key construction milestones include:
- Foundations completed by June 30, 1887.[2]
- Start of pillars/supports mounting on July 1, 1887.[1][2]
- First floor achieved on April 1, 1888 (official site) or March 20, 1888 (Wikipedia).[1][2]
- Second floor achieved on August 14, 1888 (official site) or August 21, 1888 (Wikipedia).[1][2]
- Top assembly completed on March 31, 1889.[1][2][3]

Slight discrepancies exist in dates across sources (e.g., Wikipedia lists foundations starting January 28, 1887[2]), but the official Eiffel Tower site consistently reports January 26, 1887, to March 31, 1889.[1][6] The tower opened to the public later, on May 15, 1889 (or May 6 with stairs only).[2][4] It was built for the 1889 Exposition Universelle celebrating the French Revolution's centenary.[1][4][6][7]
